overview: This is a giraffe weevil (Trachelophorus giraffa), a species endemic to the rainforests of Madagascar. The elongated neck, which is significantly longer in males, is used to fight rivals and assist females in rolling leaves into nests for their eggs. These insects are herbivores that feed on leaves and sap, and they are harmless to humans. They are relatively small, typically measuring only one to two inches in total length. The elongated neck, which is significantly longer in males, is used to fight rivals and assist females in rolling leaves into nests for their eggs.

They are relatively small, typically measuring only one to two inches in total length.
